sections.paragraph:
καθέζομαι (s. καθέδρα) impf. ἐκαθεζόμην; fut. καθεδοῦμαι and καθεσθήσομαι, 2 pl. καθεσθήσεσθε Mt 19:28 v.l. (s. κάθημαι, καθίζω); aor. ἐκαθέσθην (TestAbr A 5 p. 81, 31 [Stone p. 10]; AcPl Ha 4, 18); impv. 2 sg. καθέσθητι (TestSol 13:2); inf. καθεσθῆναι (Just., D. 83, 1) (Hom.+). In our lit., at least quite predom., it means

① to be in a seated position, sit (as Lysias 13, 37; Epict. 1, 29, 55; 3, 24, 18; Paus. 10, 5, 2; Vett. Val. 78, 24; OGI 201, 13; LXX; TestSol; Jos., Bell. 5, 73, Ant. 12, 171; Just., D. 90, 5 ἐπὶ λίθου) ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ of teachers Mt 26:55. Of pupils Lk 2:46. ἐν τῷ συνεδρίῳ in the council Ac 6:15. ἐπὶ τῆς θυρίδος 20:9 (Ael. Aristid. 47, 23 K.=23 p. 451 D.: κ. ἐπὶ βήματος). ἐπὶ τῇ πύλῃ 3:10 D. ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ sit, remain at home J 11:20. Abs. sit there 20:12. The more general be, be situated is also poss. in some pass. (Paus. Attic. ς, 8 ἐν νησίῳ καθεζόμεναι=stay; Stephan. Byz. s.v. Σκίρος: ἐν τῷ τόπῳ τούτῳ; Biogr. p. 265; Lev 12:5; Jos., Ant. 6, 32, Vi. 286; Just., D. 49, 3; 51, 2 al.).

② to take a seated position, sit down (Hom., Trag. et al.; Jos., Vi. 222; SibOr 5, 460; TestSol 13:2; 18:24; TestJob 51:3; Just., D. 107, 3.—The impf. w. aor. nuance: ‘I sat down’; B-D-F §101; s. Rob. 837ff; 882f) ἐκαθέζετο αὐτὴ ἐκ δεξιῶν she herself sat down at the right Hv 3, 2, 4. εἰς καθέδραν on a chair 3, 11, 4. Ἰησοῦς ἐκαθέζετο οὔτως ἐπὶ τῇ πηγῇ Jesus sat down, just as he was, by the well J 4:6 (on the word and the idea s. Jos., Ant. 2, 257f and Marinus, Vi. Procli 10 Boiss. As early as Demosth. 21, 119 οὑτωσὶ καθεζόμενος). Cp. also 6:3 v.l.; Lk 22:30 D. ὡς … ἐκαθέσθη as (Jerome) sat down AcPl Ha 4, 18f.—DELG s.v. ἕζομαι B2. M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pl. = plural

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

aor. aor. = aorist

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. sg. = singular

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

poss. poss. = possible

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Biogr. Biogr. = Βιογραφοι, Vitarum scriptores Gr. minores, various dates—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et et al. = et alii (and others)

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Hv Hv = Visions

Marinus, Vi. Procli Marinus, Vi. Procli = Marinus the phil., Vita Procli; V a.d.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 490
